behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on a real embedding model call, and a stub can hide real model/index dimension drift. I considered Integration, but behavior text says "embedding vector produced" (not a mocked vector), so real model output shape must be checked.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real embedding model configured in production and assert returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension before insert/search.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/query logic between search service and vector-store layer; deterministic fixtures can validate ordering. I considered System, but behavior text does not explicitly require real external contract/round-trip checks.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controlled local vector dataset with known cosine scores, execute search through service+repository layers, and assert returned ordering is strictly descending by score.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM judgment for nuanced intent; mocks always pass and cannot validate re-ranking quality/behavior change. I considered Workflow, but this behavior targets a single agentic decision point (the re-ranker step itself).
behaviors.test_description: Provide a query+candidate set where lexical similarity conflicts with intent, call the real re-ranker, and assert output order differs from raw vector ranking in expected direction.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: This is relevance-threshold calibration over model-produced semantic similarity; deterministic stubs can miss real embedding behavior that creates false positives. I considered Integration, but "no close matches" is a semantic quality condition tied to real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Using real embeddings on an adversarial out-of-domain query set and real threshold logic, assert API returns empty results (or equivalent no-match response) instead of low-score items.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline SLO across multiple sequential agentic steps (embedding + LLM rerank) plus retrieval and API path. I considered System, but behavior explicitly requires end-to-end median latency of the whole chain, which needs multi-step agentic reality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like E2E runs over representative queries through real HTTP endpoint, real vector search, real embedding call, and real re-ranker; compute median latency and assert p50 < 2.0s.
